content.full_text:
May 2, 1990

Jonathan Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Committee
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Rothman:

This letter is sent to request written confirmation of telephone advice you provided our office last week concerning the requirements of Proposition 105 (Gov. Code Sections 84501-84514). We request this written advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) on behalf of our clients "Californians for Transportation Solutions sponsored by the Planning and Conservation League -- Yes on Proposition 116" and "California Wildlife Protection Committee - A Coalition of Conservation, Outdoor and Recreation Groups - Yes on Proposition 117" ("Committees").

The Committees jointly produced an advertisement in support of Propositions 116 and 117. Each Committee paid one-half the costs of the advertisement.

You advised this office that, so long as no more than 50 per cent of the advertisement was devoted to one of the ballot measures, the Proposition 105 disclosure statement was not required.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

DIANE M. FISHBURN
